description_long: This object is a heavy-duty bench vise, typically found in workshops or garages. It features a robust, metallic body with two serrated jaws designed to securely grip objects for cutting, filing, or assembly. The main body is solid and rectangular, with a large cylindrical screw mechanism and a long handle for tightening or loosening the jaws. The vise has a dark, worn metallic finish, indicating frequent use and durability. The base is flat and wide, providing stability when mounted on a workbench. The overall construction is utilitarian, with visible bolts and a rotating spindle for precise adjustment.
description: A heavy-duty, dark metallic bench vise with serrated jaws and a long handle for tightening, designed for gripping objects securely in a workshop or garage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open jaws, main body, and the full length of the tightening handle.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular end of the screw mechanism and the handle extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the jaws open and the handle visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the vise and the mounting base.
